REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT KEVIN DORGAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART; DENIED IN PART; AND OFF CALENDAR IN PART. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PARTS OF PARAGRAPH 40 (SEE NOTICE OF MOTION). MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE CLAIM UPON WHICH A RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED (HERE, CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD) ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. REFERENCE TO THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY DOES NOT SUPPORT THE CLAIM FOR FRAUD. MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER OF TITLE. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED ON THIS ISSUE AND IT IS CURRENTLY SUBJECT TO A PENDING APPEAL. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO PARTS OF PARAGRAPH 36. (SEE NOTICE OF MOTION). MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN BY SHOWING THAT THE CLAIM FOR IIED ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. GENETHERA, INC. V. TROY & GOULD PROFESSIONAL CORP. (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 901. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ESTABLISH PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. CC 47(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Moving party failed to show that the claim upon which a relief may be granted (here, cause of action for fraud) arises out of protected activity.
